name: Lahij
description: Lahij was the seat of the Abdali Sultanate — independent until 1839 — and their Hisn al-Abdali palace complex combines Yemeni courtyard tradition with Ottoman and later British colonial influence. The Sultanate's architectural patronage extended across a network of mosques and market buildings in the Tihama transitional zone.
traditional: Courtyard palace with arcaded perimeter, coral block walls, decorated reception hall.
transitional: Courtyard plan retained; local materials combined with colonial brick detailing.
contemporary: Contemporary Lahij: courtyard typology with modern services; arcade as thermal device.
